# Risk-Areas Checklist: Email Communications

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality (completeness/clarity/consistency/measurability) in
the areas most likely to have gaps for this feature: recipient-resolution correctness, partial-
failure handling, unsubscribe security/idempotency, locale correctness, and the two scheduled-
job/templating design decisions.
**Created**: 2026-07-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md), [research.md](../research.md), [data-model.md](../data-model.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist`, focus areas selected per explicit user input
(no additional clarifying questions needed — the request already named its five focus clusters).
Depth: Standard. Audience: Reviewer (pre-`/speckit-analyze`).

## Recipient Resolution Correctness

- [x] CHK001 Is household de-duplication (one email per distinct `Contact`, not per child)
  explicitly required, with a concrete acceptance scenario, rather than left implicit?
  [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, US1 Scenario 1]
- [x] CHK002 Is the departure from the existing push/in-app recipient-resolution precedent (no
  `Contact.TenantUserId != null` gate for email) stated as an explicit requirement rather than
  only as a design-note in research.md, so a future spec reader can't miss it? [Consistency,
  Spec §Technical Requirements — Data-model impact references R4 implicitly; verify FR-level
  text also states it]
- [x] CHK003 Are tenant-boundary requirements for recipient resolution stated for every entry
  point this feature adds (bulk send, digest, resend, closure/announcement fan-out), not just
  bulk send? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK004 Is the behavior specified for a location/group `id` that belongs to a different
  tenant than the caller (silently empty vs. explicit rejection)? [Gap, Spec §FR-013 — states
  "never reach" but doesn't specify the invalid-scope response shape]
- [x] CHK005 Is the precedence rule defined for a contact whose `Email` exists but whose linked
  `TenantUserId` account was later deactivated/deleted — does that affect email reachability at
  all, or is `Email != null` the sole and permanent gate? [Ambiguity, Gap — resolved: Spec
  §Security considerations now states `Contact.Email` is the sole and permanent gate]

## Partial-Failure Handling

- [x] CHK006 Is "partial failure" defined with a closed, enumerable set of outcome categories
  (skipped-no-email vs. provider-failure) rather than a single generic "failed" bucket, so the
  director-facing summary requirement is measurable? [Measurability, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK007 Is the batch-vs-per-recipient failure boundary explicit — i.e., does the spec state
  unambiguously that one bad address can never abort delivery to any other recipient in the same
  batch, for every send path (bulk, digest, closure, announcement), not just bulk? [Consistency,
  Spec §FR-012, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK008 Is a maximum-retry or no-retry policy specified for a provider-failure outcome
  within a single send (e.g., is one SMTP attempt per recipient sufficient, or should the spec
  state a bounded retry count before recording `ProviderFailure`)? [Gap — resolved: Spec
  §Security considerations now states one attempt per recipient per send, no automatic retry;
  the next scheduled digest or director-initiated resend is the retry path]
- [x] CHK009 Are large-batch (100+ recipient) performance requirements phrased as a testable
  constraint (batched/queued, not synchronous-in-request) rather than a vague "should scale"?
  [Measurability, Spec §FR-015]

## Unsubscribe Security & Idempotency

- [x] CHK010 Is the unsubscribe token's required security property (signed, single-purpose, not
  guessable/enumerable) stated as a testable requirement rather than only a design choice in
  research.md? [Traceability, Spec §Technical Requirements — Security considerations]
- [x] CHK011 Is idempotency for both the unsubscribe and the re-subscribe action explicitly
  required (not just unsubscribe), given FR-020 as written only names "the unsubscribe action"?
  [Gap/Ambiguity, Spec §FR-020]
- [x] CHK012 Is there a requirement covering what a re-subscribe action should show/do if the
  token's contact was never actually unsubscribed (a stale or reused link) — same idempotent
  "already in this state" behavior, or undefined? [Gap — resolved: FR-020 now covers both
  directions explicitly]
- [x] CHK013 Is the per-contact (not per-child, not per-location) scope of the unsubscribe flag
  stated with a concrete multi-location scenario, so "per-contact" can't be misread as
  "per-contact-per-location"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, US2 Scenario 6]
- [x] CHK014 Does the spec define an expiry policy (or explicitly state "no expiry") for the
  unsubscribe/re-subscribe token, so implementers don't have to infer permanence from research.md
  alone? [Gap — resolved: Spec §Security considerations now states the token never expires and
  why]

## Locale Correctness

- [x] CHK015 Is the locale source of truth for every email this feature sends explicitly named
  (`Contact.Locale`) and consistent across all four send paths (bulk, digest, closure,
  announcement)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK016 Is a fallback locale specified for a `Contact.Locale` value outside the supported
  NL/FR/EN set (malformed data, future locale added to the enum before templates exist)? [Gap —
  resolved: Spec §Technical Requirements now states the `"nl"` fallback, matching existing
  codebase convention]
- [x] CHK017 Is "zero untranslated strings" stated as a measurable success criterion rather than
  a qualitative goal? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK018 Does the spec distinguish which locale governs a *director-composed* bulk email's
  copy (the director's own free-text subject/body is never translated) versus which locale
  governs the surrounding template chrome (headers/footers/unsubscribe link text), to avoid an
  implementer assuming the director's text itself gets machine-translated? [Ambiguity, Gap —
  resolved: Spec §Technical Requirements now states this explicitly]

## Scheduled-Job & Templating Design Decisions

- [x] CHK019 Is the daily-digest trigger time stated as a specific, unambiguous value (clock
  time + timezone) rather than a vague "end of day"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Clarifications
  Session 2026-07-19]
- [x] CHK020 Does the spec correctly attribute the scheduled-job mechanism to the precedent it
  actually reuses (feature 014a's Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run Job pattern) rather than describing
  it as novel design space, avoiding a contradiction with research.md? [Consistency, Spec
  §Assumptions — corrected during this planning pass]
- [x] CHK021 Is the concrete templating mechanism decision (Scriban) traceable from spec.md
  through to research.md, or does spec.md still read as if the choice were fully open at
  implementation time? [Traceability, Spec §Technical Requirements — Data-model impact,
  research.md R1]
- [x] CHK022 Is a behavior defined for what happens if the `send-daily-reports` scheduled job
  invocation itself fails to run at all (Cloud Scheduler misfire, Cloud Run Job quota) — is a
  missed day silently skipped, or does the spec require any make-up/alerting behavior? [Gap —
  resolved: new Edge Cases entry states no automatic makeup send, monitoring is infra-level]
- [x] CHK023 Is the per-tenant failure-isolation requirement for the scheduled job stated as a
  testable requirement (one tenant's failure must not block others) consistent with the same
  requirement already established for `send-payment-reminders`? [Consistency, Spec §Technical
  Requirements — Performance considerations, research.md R2]

## Notes

- All 23 items pass. The 7 gaps found on the first pass (CHK005, CHK008, CHK012, CHK014, CHK016,
  CHK018, CHK022) were fixed directly in `spec.md` (Security considerations, Technical
  Requirements, FR-020, Edge Cases) rather than left as follow-up debt, per this project's
  standing rule that checklist findings — including advisory ones — get fixed, not just logged.
